reforms.description: The Government of Korea will provide customized support for digital manufacturing innovation to 25,000 small and medium-sized manufacturing companies by 2027, aimed at advancing smart factories through public-private collaboration. Additionally, Korea is streamlining visa requirements for foreign entrepreneurs to enhance the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The support for digital manufacturing and streamlined visa requirements for entrepreneurs are expected to enhance innovation and productivity in the manufacturing and startup sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem and supporting digital manufacturing.
reforms.source_quote: Korea should streamline visa requirements for entrepreneurs (D-8-4). Educational criteria should be reconsidered, given that ground-breaking ideas, rather than formal education, can lead to the establishment of unicorn startups.

reforms.description: Korea launched the AI Strategy High-Level Consultative Council in April 2024 to enhance its national strategy for artificial intelligence, expanding the number of AI Engineering schools from 5 to 19 since 2019.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The expansion of AI Engineering schools is expected to build a skilled workforce in AI, enhancing innovation and productivity in the technology sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the council and expansion of schools represent significant efforts to enhance human capital in AI.
reforms.source_quote: Korea launched the AI Strategy High-Level Consultative Council in April 2024. The number of AI Engineering schools has expanded from 5 in 2019 to 19 in early 2024.

reforms.description: The government plans to extend the maximum period of parental leave per parent by another six months, with a flexible structure allowing higher benefits for the initial months and lower levels for subsequent periods.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact on economic growth is ambiguous as it may support family welfare but could also affect labor market participation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform is a minor adjustment to existing parental leave policies.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to extend the maximum period of parental leave per parent by another six months.

reforms.description: The Korean government plans to expand the inflow of foreign workers by increasing visa quotas and centralizing immigration policies, aiming to address labor shortages and enhance economic productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the inflow of foreign workers is expected to alleviate labor shortages and enhance productivity in various sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in immigration policy aimed at addressing labor market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to expand the inflow of foreign workers notably through expanding visa quotas as part of comprehensive immigration policies.

reforms.description: The government is expanding on-site Korean language courses for foreign workers, aiming to improve their integration into the labor market and enhance their employability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving language skills for foreign workers is expected to enhance their employability and integration into the labor market, supporting economic productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The expansion of language courses is a minor adjustment to existing integration programs.
reforms.source_quote: The government has been offering on-site Korean language courses to foreign workers in the shipbuilding industry.
